column2: Due to a shortage of consumer goods on the home front, the troops are worried about their families and loved ones. This is reducing their effectiveness in combat.
The leader of these units is using his skills to good effect.
Our choice of difficulty level is affecting combat.
Units that are encircled fight at a reduced efficiency.
Due to attacks coming from multiple directions, these units are fighting at reduced efficiency.
Each brigade committed into combat demands increasing staff work to coordinate properly. As more brigades are committed, the overall efficiency of the forces drops.
The more experienced a brigade is, the better it will perform in combat.
Doctrine research gives bonuses to certain missions, allowing the units to perform better.
Units will use time not moving and fighting to dig in. Dug-in units defend better due to their better defensive positions.
When attempting an amphibious assault, the attacking units fight at a reduced rate. This is due to the limited number of possible landing sites, which allow the defender to focus his efforts there.
Prepared fortifications give the defenders excellent cover, meaning the attacker fights at reduced efficiency.
Nearby Headquarters take some of the command burden off your generals, allowing them to focus on the battle and leading to greater combat efficiency.
Dropping units by parachute is a very risky business. Inevitably parts of the unit will be dispersed over a wide area, leading to reduced combat performance.
Rivers make excellent defensive positions due to the difficulty of crossing them. This terrain favours the defence.
The supporting big guns of the adjacent battle fleet are making things a little uncomfortable for the opposition.
With insufficient supplies reaching the units they are having to be careful in their use of things like ammunition. This is reducing their effectiveness.
Units that are simultaneously attacking and defending find it a bit difficult to do both.
A division with a blend of hard units (like armour) and soft units performs much better than other divisions.
An air unit operating in close proximity to its base will be carrying more fuel and ammunition, allowing it greater effectiveness in combat.
Nearby radar stations can provide information on enemy air units, improving efficiency in air combat.
Fighter units dedicated to air superiority or intercept missions receive bonuses in air-to-air combat.
Enemy fighter units are impeding this unit’s ability to bomb, reducing its overall effectiveness.
The terrain in this province is affecting the combat
The weather in this province is affecting the combat.
Fighting at night is very disorienting to troops. Problems recognising friendly troops and just finding their way around reduce combat efficiency
Units caught by surprise do not defend as well, due to their lack of preparation.
Units fighting for land they believe is theirs fight much harder for it.
Superior radio communications allows greater coordination amongst the various s of the brigade, improving their overall efficiency in combat.
A single brigade cannot have a general to command it in battle.
Expeditionary force returned to owner on §R$DATE$§W
